name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-17
title: Hooks do not signal failure to harness; subprocess failures are swallowed by sync-check / session-init bare except
description: All four hooks (scope-guard.py, sync-check.py, session-init.py, refresh.py) follow the pattern 'except Exception: ...' silently swallowing subprocess errors, JSON decode errors, file read failures. The Claude Code hook contract is to exit 0 (no message) or non-zero (deny). But silently exiting 0 when an internal failure occurs (git invocation fails, generate script fails, registry missing) means the user gets no signal that policy enforcement has effectively become a no-op. Add structured stderr logging for unexpected exceptions while keeping the 'happy path quiet' contract.
